summary: Pipeline Health Badge Feature - All 18 backend tests PASSED (100%), All frontend tests PASSED. Batch metrics endpoint and Pipeline health badges correctly implemented.
critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED: batch-metrics returns 401 for unauthenticated requests
VERIFIED: batch-metrics returns 403 for non-athlete roles (admin, director, coach)
VERIFIED: batch-metrics caps at 50 program_ids (tested with 63 IDs)
VERIFIED: batch-metrics handles missing/invalid program_ids gracefully (returns _error metadata)
VERIFIED: Pipeline page loads and renders Kanban board with health badges on school cards
VERIFIED: Health badges have data-testid='health-badge-{program_id}' pattern
VERIFIED: UF card shows 'Strong Momentum' badge (green) - Correct
VERIFIED: Stanford card shows 'Cooling Off' badge (orange) - Correct
VERIFIED: UCLA card shows 'Active' badge (blue) - Correct
VERIFIED: Total 12 health badges rendered on 12 Kanban cards
VERIFIED: Previous Pipeline functionality intact (hero card, drag-drop, navigation)
CODE REVIEW: _resolve_tenant() is duplicated in program_metrics.py (lines 23-32 and 55-64) - minor cleanup opportunity
seed_data_creation: None - used existing Emma Chen data with 12 programs
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pipeline health badge feature fully tested. New batch-metrics endpoint (POST /api/internal/programs/batch-metrics) accepts program_ids array, returns metrics dict keyed by program_id. PipelineHealthBadge component renders compact badges with icon+label. Badge states: strong_momentum (green), active (blue), needs_follow_up (amber), cooling_off (orange), at_risk (red). Tooltip shows explanation from last meaningful engagement. Frontend fetches batch-metrics after programs load, passes healthMap to KanbanBoard, KanbanCard receives healthMetrics prop. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_batch_metrics.py (18 tests). Total iterations: 94.
